analytical-evaluator.description: Seeks to understand all variables before deciding. Responds to data, structured comparisons, and logical reasoning. Will ask about every cost line.
common_objections: what happens in the worst months?
what_works: explicit cost breakdown (management fee, utilities, cleaning)
analytical-evaluator.follow_up_cadence: 3-5 days — give time to review but not so long they go cold

certainty-seeker.description: Needs predictability more than income maximisation. Will sacrifice upside for consistency. Worst-month question is the real close.
what_works: payout record (1st-5th every month, never missed)
offer as certainty anchor — locks in the lower fee permanently
certainty-seeker.typical_outcome: special_offer_applied — offer functions as certainty bridge
certainty-seeker.follow_up_cadence: 2-3 days — they have a decision timeline in mind, move alongside it

loss-averse-switcher.description: Previously used an STL manager they're unhappy with. Core fear: making the same mistake with a new provider. Income is secondary to reliability.
common_objections: how are you different from [previous provider]?
I don't want to go through this again
what_works: acknowledge the specific failure of the previous provider
show exactly how Stayful handles that exact scenario differently
named accountability — they deal with a specific person, not a call centre
damage reporting: within 24h, photographic evidence, insurance process
what_fails: pivoting to income before trust is established
generic 'we're better' claims with no specifics
minimising or dismissing their past experience
loss-averse-switcher.typical_outcome: special_offer_applied when trust issue is directly addressed
loss-averse-switcher.follow_up_cadence: 24-48h — strike while trust window is open

betrayal-damaged.description: Deeper version of loss-averse. Feels personally let down by specific broken promises. 'They promised £1,500-2,000 and we got £1,000.' Income claim damage is the wound.
common_objections: they promised X and delivered Y
I don't want to make another mistake with another company
the June booking shows it can work but I need to know you can sustain it
what_works: acknowledge the specific broken promise without excusing it
explain exactly what caused the underperformance (reviews maturity, listing quality)
show what Stayful does at that exact failure point
offer as trust bridge — lower fee reduces perceived risk of trying again
betrayal-damaged.follow_up_cadence: 3-5 days — need time to process trust conversation

urgency-driven.description: Active financial pressure or vacancy cost is the primary driver. Every week of delay has a price tag they already know.
what_works: fast-track setup narrative (2-3 weeks from agreement to live)
compliance checklist so they know what to action
what_fails: adding friction to the onboarding process
urgency-driven.typical_outcome: special_offer_applied — moves fast when gates are cleared
urgency-driven.follow_up_cadence: same day or next morning — urgency profile expects speed

fast-path-gain-focused.description: Excited by the income opportunity. Already researching multiple providers. Low friction, high intent. Will move fast if Stayful moves fast.
what_works: offer to differentiate on value not just price
what_fails: under-delivering on projected figures in Year 1
fast-path-gain-focused.typical_outcome: special_offer_applied or lost to competitor — window is short
fast-path-gain-focused.follow_up_cadence: within 24h — competitor is also following up

social-proof-dependent.description: Needs to hear from similar property owners before deciding. Reviews and case studies are primary close tools.
common_objections: can I speak to someone who uses you?
what have other similar owners done?

status-quo-resistant.description: Actively resisting change even when numbers work. Inertia is the primary barrier.
